concepts_used.title: 加权求和评价
concepts_used.how_used: 论文将经济效率、碳储存潜力、环境社会影响三个指标标准化为5分制，然后等权相加得到偏好系数P，用于综合评价各封存方法。
concepts_used.source_excerpt: The preferability coefficient, P, is the sum of the economic rating, the sequestration potential, and the societal rating and is used to evaluate the overall advantages of each method. ... P_n = E_n + S_n + C_n

concepts_used.title: 线性规划建模
concepts_used.how_used: 论文建立了线性规划模型，以最大化总偏好得分Z为目标，决策变量为每种封存方法的年封存量Q_n，并受技术可行性约束。
concepts_used.source_excerpt: The purpose of the model was to manipulate each Q_n to maximize the preferability score, Z, of a given scenario.

concepts_used.title: 灵敏度分析
concepts_used.how_used: 论文通过改变约束条件（如预算、碳中和目标）来测试不同情景下的最优解，分析模型对参数变化的敏感性。
concepts_used.source_excerpt: Our model evaluated three scenarios that projected either the cost of carbon neutrality, the maximum capacity given the proposed 2009 budget, or the sequestration rates needed to maximize preferability.

concepts_used.title: 模型假设的写法
concepts_used.how_used: 论文明确列出了关键假设，如各地质封存方法成本无显著差异、陆地封存容量估算基于年封存率等，并说明假设的合理性。
concepts_used.source_excerpt: Based on available data, we assumed that the cost between each geological method was not significantly different. ... we assumed that this estimation realistically represents the capacity of terrestrial sequestration relative to the other methods.

concepts_used.title: 图表标题与编号规范
concepts_used.how_used: 论文使用三线表规范呈现数据，表格有编号和标题，如Table 1: Cost for Each Method，并引用附录中的详细数据。
concepts_used.source_excerpt: Table 1: Cost for Each Method ... Please see Appendix Table 1

concepts_used.title: 中心化/归一化/标准化
concepts_used.how_used: 论文将各指标原始值标准化到1-5分制，使不同量纲的指标可比较，例如经济评分通过成本/吨转换为吨/100美元后按比例标准化。
concepts_used.source_excerpt: The economic rating was found by converting the cost/ton into tons/$100 and then standardized so the highest rating was five and the others followed proportionally.

concepts_used.title: 附录组织
concepts_used.how_used: 论文在正文中多次引用附录中的详细计算和表格，如Appendix Calculations for Detailed Explanation of Estimates，以保持正文简洁。
concepts_used.source_excerpt: Please see Appendix Calculations for Detailed Explanation of Estimates. ... Please see Appendix Tables 2 and 3
